kept.text:
Day 82 of learning how to script on Roblox! 👀✨

Kept it super simple tonight since I'm currently dealing with food poisoning (DON'T EAT FOOD FROM TIKTOK) 🥲 So here's a script I came up with that I made completely on my own without needing to look back on anything! 

Of course I would not use this method for an official game and I would use server storage/destroy to reduce any possible lag. However, to keep is simple enough for me to actually challenge my full memory I used if/elseif statements for this one and lots of transparency prompts! 

I'm really hoping tomorrow I don't feel that bad so I can continue learning some new things I haven't gotten to yet with BrawlDevs advanced scripting series such as drag detection and raycasting 🥹

kept.text:
if you know anything abt modern IPv4 infrastructure, Roblox's "alt account detection" by IP address is a *really* stupid idea that gets tons of false positives, here's why:

on most wifi connections, you get what is known as a "private" IPv4 address assigned to your device. this address by itself is *not* directly routable on the public internet, so it goes through a process called Network Address Translation (NAT). this takes your private IP (192.168.x.x or similar) and makes it a public IP which can be directly sent across the internet. Roblox never sees your private IP via this process, since every device on the local network gets the same translated public IP in most cases. Basically this means if there are 1000 accounts on the same network (i.e. a college or coffee shop network), they all can share the same public IP and in Roblox's eyes, look like they belong to one user. they then associate these accounts to one user, and ban all accounts that use the same network if one account breaks ToS.

you end up w potentially 100s or 1000s of accounts being false flagged because they share the same IP at a public location like airport or college or coffee shop when one user does something bad

this doesn't even cover larger CG-NAT networks which are common in europe and asia where even more ppl share 1 public IP...

with IPv6, this is less of an issue since each device gets a unique global address, but roblox doesn't support IPv6 last I checked and i dont think they plan to anytime soon

anyways thanks for coming to my ted talk hopefully you learned something new :3

We’re on the verge of interactive, real-time, photorealistic video generation with what are called World Models. These require a fair amount of expensive compute, but costs will come down over time. Today, these interactions are essentially real-time dreams. They lack the persistence and shared logic that turns a video into a multiplayer game. Or concert. Or classroom. Or the holodeck.

We believe the ultimate architecture for gaming (and the holodeck) is Roblox Reality. It’s a hybrid architecture that marries the structured data and logic of the Roblox Engine and Roblox Cloud with the generative power of Video World Models. The Roblox Engine provides the underlying synchronized ground truth—the score, physics, multiplayer sync, etc.—while our video model acts as a Super Upsampler to layer on photorealistic detail.

We believe this will ultimately remove barriers to high-fidelity creation, allowing a team of three people to build a narrative-driven, photorealistic masterpiece in a single week. This is an early look at turning solitary AI dreams into a social, playable reality.
